name: Lemon (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh lemon flesh (without peel)

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Wang et al. 2021 (fresh lemon juice, HPLC) doi:10.3390/foods10102384 (PMC8588450); citric 51.46 g/L. Cross-checked Penniston 2008 (48-60 g/L).
components.evidence: DOMINANT carbon/energy source of lemon (>90% of total acids). Juice basis 51.46 g/L = 5.15 g/100mL; whole-flesh value ~4.7-5.0 g/100g.

components.name: L-malic acid
components.source: Wang et al. 2021 PMC8588450 (fresh lemon juice HPLC); malic 5.80 g/L.
components.evidence: Second organic acid; commonly missing from FooDB profile.

components.name: succinic acid
components.source: Wang et al. 2021 PMC8588450 (fresh lemon juice HPLC); succinic 1.19 g/L.

components.name: oxalic acid
components.source: Wang et al. 2021 PMC8588450 (fresh lemon juice HPLC); oxalic 1.03 g/L.

components.name: lactic acid
components.source: Wang et al. 2021 PMC8588450 (fresh lemon juice HPLC); lactic 0.61 g/L.

components.name: D-glucose
components.source: USDA FDC 167747 (lemon juice, raw): glucose 0.99 g/100g. Applied to flesh (total sugars 2.50 g/100g flesh ~ 2.52 g juice).
components.evidence: Individual fermentable sugar; FooDB had no amount.

components.name: D-fructose
components.source: USDA FDC 167747 (lemon juice, raw): fructose 1.10 g/100g.
components.evidence: Most abundant free sugar in lemon.

components.name: sucrose
components.source: USDA FDC 167747 (lemon juice, raw): sucrose 0.43 g/100g.
components.evidence: Third fermentable sugar. Total sugars USDA FDC 167746 flesh = 2.50 g/100g (glc+fru+suc = 2.50).

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: Bulk amino-acid nitrogen source. USDA lemon has no per-amino-acid panel; free amino acids present (asparagine, proline, serine, aspartate, glutamate dominant in citrus) but unquantified in authoritative databases - listed below as null.

components.name: L-aspartate
components.evidence: Present; no authoritative amount for lemon flesh.

components.name: L-asparagine
components.source: Citrus free-AA literature (dominant free AA in citrus juice)
components.evidence: Present; typically the major free amino acid in citrus, unquantified for lemon flesh.

components.name: ascorbic acid (vitamin C)
components.evidence: Vitamin C and a minor organic acid; defining lemon nutrient.

components.name: biotin (B7)
components.source: FooDB FOOD00054 (present, not in USDA lemon panel)

components.name: iron
components.evidence: Single consolidated Fe entry (FooDB had 3 redundant iron rows).

components.name: linoleic acid (18:2 n-6)
components.evidence: Major fatty acid (total lipid only 0.30 g).

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace contaminant, absent from USDA lemon panel; not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: cobalt ion trace/contaminant; lemon has no B12 and Co is not a characterized lemon nutrient; FooDB artifact

remove.name: Iodine c
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace element, negligible/absent in lemon flesh; FooDB placeholder

remove.name: Chromium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace element, absent from USDA lemon panel; FooDB noise

remove.name: Molybdate
remove.reason: trace element not quantified for lemon; not characteristic; FooDB placeholder

remove.name: CO2 CO2
remove.reason: metabolic/atmospheric gas, not a food nutrient; FooDB environment artifact

remove.name: O2 O2
remove.reason: atmospheric gas, environment not food composition; FooDB artifact

remove.name: Nitrogen
remove.reason: N2 gas, not a bioavailable nutrient of the food; FooDB artifact

remove.name: Fe2+ mitochondria
remove.reason: subcellular-compartment/duplicate iron entry; consolidated into single 'iron' 0.60 mg

remove.name: Iron (Fe3+)
remove.reason: redundant duplicate iron entry; consolidated into single 'iron'

remove.name: Pentadecanoic acid
remove.reason: odd-chain C15:0 (ruminant/dairy fat marker); implausible/negligible in a plant food; FooDB noise

remove.name: Decanal C10H20O
remove.reason: peel essential-oil volatile aldehyde; trace aroma, not a growth nutrient (food is flesh without peel)

remove.name: Dodecanal C12H24O
remove.reason: peel essential-oil volatile aldehyde; trace aroma, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Heptanal C7H14O
remove.reason: volatile aldehyde; trace aroma compound, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Nonanal C9H18O
remove.reason: volatile aldehyde; trace aroma compound, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Decanoic acid
remove.reason: trace peel/oil medium-chain fatty acid; negligible in lemon flesh (total lipid 0.3 g)

remove.name: Dodecanoic acid
remove.reason: trace peel/oil fatty acid (lauric); negligible in lemon flesh

missing_added: citric acid (5.0 g/100g - dominant carbon source, was absent)
quantified individual sugars: D-glucose 0.98, D-fructose 1.09, sucrose 0.43 g/100g
full mineral panel quantified from USDA
keep_uncovered: genuine citrus flavonoids/limonoids/terpenes - real phytochemicals, no BiGG metabolite
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 167746 (Lemons, raw, without peel) - macros, minerals, vitamins, fatty acids, total sugars
USDA FoodData Central FDC 167747 (Lemon juice, raw) - individual sugars glucose/fructose/sucrose
Wang et al. 2021, Foods 10(10):2384, doi:10.3390/foods10102384 (PMC8588450) - HPLC organic acids of fresh lemon juice (citric 51.46, malic 5.80, succinic 1.19, oxalic 1.03, lactic 0.61 g/L)
Penniston et al. 2008 (citric acid in lemon/lime juice, 48-60 g/L) - cross-check
FooDB FOOD00054 (compound presence for phytochemicals and free amino acids)
notes: Dominant carbon/energy source is citric acid (~5 g/100g, >90% of total acids), followed by the fermentable sugars fructose > glucose > sucrose (total ~2.5 g/100g) and secondary organic acids (malic, succinic, oxalic, lactic). Organic acids were the key FooDB omission and were added from peer-reviewed HPLC data (juice basis; whole-flesh values comparable). Nitrogen is supplied mainly as 1.1 g/100g protein plus free amino acids (asparagine/proline/serine/aspartate/glutamate dominant in citrus, unquantified). Removed FooDB noise: trace metals (Ni, Co, I, Cr, Mo), atmospheric gases (CO2, O2, N2), duplicate/compartment iron rows, an implausible odd-chain dairy-marker fatty acid, and peel essential-oil volatiles (food is flesh without peel).
